Here the commentator raises a doubt whether the expression
height refers to the door or the object that pierces or wounds.
Here it is Garga*s statement, more than that of the W^ff^cTT, that
dispels the doubt.]

A door wounded by a road presages the owner's death; by a
tree, harm to children; by mire, grief; by a runnel, excessive expen-
diture of money; by a well, epilepsy; by an idol of God, the owner's
destruction; by a pillar, frailty of women; and one facing Brahman,
extinction of the family.
